content.description:
Container views now handle open-item intents directly, instead of `openItem` bypassing the container and going straight to `kernel.attach`.

## The Problem

`openItem` used to call `kernel.attach(target, itemIdOrAttachment)` directly. This caused two issues:

1. **`kernel.attach` clobbers** — replaces the entire attachment, losing position (`x`, `y`, `w`, `h`). This was deliberate (commit `208350d`) to fix minimized items not restoring, but it's a blunt fix.
2. **No way to update `navigateTo` on an already-visible item** — morphdom skipped re-rendering because `data-child-item` and `data-child-view` hadn't changed.

Every attempted fix leaked canvas knowledge (`minimized`, `z`, position) into the viewport layer.

## Solution: `:open-item` on `:render-child`

Container views provide an `:open-item` handler function on their `:render-child` elements. This function receives the `itemIdOrAttachment` argument and handles attach/bring-to-front/unminimize/navigateTo with full knowledge of its own schema.

### How it works

1. **`openItem`** (viewport-rendering) handles Ctrl/Alt modifiers, then delegates to the container's handler via `context.openItemFn`. Falls back to the old `openIn`/attach path if no handler.
2. **Container handler** (e.g. spatial canvas) does an atomic mutation: checks if already attached, unminimizes, brings to front, sets navigateTo — one `set-item!` call, one re-render.
3. **Morphdom** compares `data-navigate-to` attribute alongside `data-child-item`/`data-child-view`. When navigateTo changes, the child re-renders and scrolls to the symbol.
4. **Selector** includes `navigateTo` so the reactive system triggers re-render when it changes.

### What changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| [viewport-rendering](item://f1111111-0008-0000-0000-000000000000) | `openItem` delegates to `context.openItemFn`; morphdom compares `data-navigate-to`; `fillMountPoints` inherits `openItemFn` from parent context; instance stores/recovers `openItemFn` |
| [hob-interpreter](item://40b00001-0000-4000-8000-000000000000) | Parses `:open-item` on `:render-child`; sets `data-navigate-to` attribute |
| [spatial-canvas-view](item://ef793c27-2d4b-4c99-b05a-2769db5bc5a9) | `open-item-handler` function replaces `:open-in`; selector includes `navigateTo` |

### Backwards compatible

Views that still use `:open-in` (sortable-list, modal-frame, error-list, app-page) continue working via the fallback path. They can be migrated to `:open-item` later.

### Relation to `siblingContainer`

This replaces the same need that `siblingContainer` (removed in `5e04db8`) addressed — letting the container control how items are opened within it. But instead of threading invisible capability objects through the render tree, the handler is an explicit function on `:render-child` that inherits through context.

## See Also

- [Auto-Generated Diagrams](item://c0c0c0c0-005a-0000-0000-000000000000) — Where this was discovered
- [System Layers](item://c0c0c0c0-0091-0000-0000-000000000000) — Layer boundaries
- [Event System](item://c0c0c0c0-0006-0000-0000-000000000000) — Declarative watches
